13.reason: The condition incorrectly requires all three character range checks to be true simultaneously, which is impossible. It should use logical OR (||) to allow any one of the three ranges (lowercase, uppercase, digits) to be valid for expansion.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.5 , reason:  The condition incorrectly requires all three character type ranges to be true simultaneously, but only one type should be checked. Also, the condition uses <= and >= incorrectly for range checks (e.g., 'a' to 'z' should be s[i-1] >= 'a' && s[i+1] <= 'z').
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.5 , reason:  The condition incorrectly requires all three character type ranges to be true simultaneously, but only one type should be checked. Also, the range checks are wrong (e.g., 'Z' is less than 'a', so this condition will never be true for valid expansions).
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 1.0 , reason:  The condition incorrectly requires all three character range checks to be true simultaneously, which is impossible. It should use logical OR (||) to allow any one of the three ranges (lowercase, uppercase, digits) to be valid for expansion.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 1.0 , reason:  [Error: The condition uses '&&' between three mutually exclusive range checks (uppercase, digit, lowercase). This makes the condition always false because a character cannot be in all three ranges simultaneously. Should use '||' to check each type separately, and also ensure both characters are within the same full range, not just lower bound for left and upper bound for right.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 1.0 , reason:  The logical operators && between the three character range checks should be ||, because the characters around '-' must belong to the same type (either all lowercase, all uppercase, or all digits), not simultaneously satisfy all three ranges.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 1.0 , reason:  The condition incorrectly uses && to combine mutually exclusive character range checks, making it logically impossible to be true. It should use || to check if both characters are lowercase, uppercase, or digits respectively, e.g., ((s[i-1]>='a'&&s[i+1]<='z')||(s[i-1]>='A'&&s[i+1]<='Z')||(s[i-1]>='0'&&s[i+1]<='9')).
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 1.0 , reason:  [The condition uses && between all type checks, which means it requires the characters to simultaneously be uppercase, digit, and lowercase — impossible. It should use || to check if they belong to any one of the valid same-type categories. Also, boundary checks are incorrect: e.g., (s[i+1]<='Z' && s[i-1]>='A') doesn't ensure both are uppercase; it allows mixed cases like '0' and 'Z'.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 1.0 , reason:  [The condition uses && between all type checks, which means it requires the characters to simultaneously be uppercase, digit, and lowercase — impossible. It should use || to check if they belong to any one of the valid same-type ranges. Also, it doesn't verify that both characters are of the same category (e.g., both uppercase), leading to incorrect rejection of valid expansions like 'B-F'.]

25.reason: When expansion occurs, the '-' character is still printed because the else branch prints s[i] for all cases, including when s[i] is '-' and expansion is performed. The '-' should be skipped when expansion happens.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.5 , reason:  When expansion occurs, the '-' character is still printed because the else branch prints s[i] for all cases, including when s[i] is '-' and expansion is performed. The '-' should be skipped when expansion happens.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.5 , reason:  This prints the '-' character when expansion is performed, but the '-' should not be printed. Also, when expansion is performed, the characters before and after '-' are not printed correctly (the start character is missing, and the end character is printed later by this else branch).
